Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With reference to the Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry (APAAR), consider the following statements:
1. It is a scheme launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) under the ‘Digital India’ program.
2. It provides a unique 12-digit ID that consolidates a student’s academic and co-curricular achievements throughout their educational journey.
3. Parental consent is mandatory for creating an APAAR ID for a minor student.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• APAAR is launched by the Ministry of Education, not by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
• APAAR provides a unique 12-digit ID that consolidates a student’s academic and co-curricular achievements throughout their educational journey.
• Parental consent is mandatory for creating an APAAR ID for a minor student.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The E-Jagriti platform, recently seen in the news, is related to which of the following?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• E-Jagriti is a digital platform developed by the Department of Consumer Affairs, Government
of India, to unify and digitize the entire consumer grievance redressal process across National,
State, Circuit Bench, and District Consumer Commissions.
• The platform integrates multiple legacy systems into one modern, AI and digital technologypowered
portal that allows filing consumer complaints, online fee payment, case monitoring,
virtual hearings, and smart search of case data.

Correct answer: C
Solution

defence system to be fully developed by 2035.

initiative to build a robust indigenous shield against aerial threats.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

With reference to the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic
Cooperation (BIMSTEC), consider the following statements:
1. BIMSTEC was established through the Bangkok Declaration in 1997.
2. Bangladesh, India and Malaysia are among the founding members.
3. The BIMSTEC Secretariat is located in Dhaka, Bangladesh.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
(BIMSTEC) is a grouping of seven Member States lying in the littoral and adjacent areas of
the Bay of Bengal.
• The organization was formed in 1997 with the signing of the Bangkok Declaration.
• Founding Members (1997): Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka, Thailand.

Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

National Space Day is observed in India to commemorate which of the following events?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• India celebrated its second National Space Day on August 23, 2025 with the theme “Aryabhatta
to Gaganyaan: Ancient Wisdom to Infinite Possibilities”.
• August 23rd was declared as “National Space Day” to honour the success of the Chandrayaan-
3 mission, which achieved a safe and soft landing of the Vikram Lander at the ‘Shiv Shakti’
point and deployed the Pragyaan Rover on the lunar surface.

Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Euthanasia:
1. Active Euthanasia refers to death by not providing essential, necessary and ordinary care or food and water.
2. Passive Euthanasia refers to causing intentional death of a human being by direct intervention.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Active Euthanasia: It is also known as ‘Positive Euthanasia’ or ‘Aggressive Euthanasia’ refers
to causing intentional death of a human being by direct intervention. It is a direct action
performed to end useless life and a meaningless existence.
• Passive Euthanasia: It is also known as ‘Negative Euthanasia’ or ‘Non-Aggressive Euthanasia’.
It is intentionally causing death by not providing essential, necessary and ordinary care or
food and water.

Q16 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Consider the following statements regarding Conference of the Parties (COP) to United Nations
Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC):
1. The COP is the supreme decision-making body of the UNFCCC.
2. COP assesses the progress made in achieving the ultimate objective of the Convention.
3. Not all states that are parties to the UNFCCC are represented at the COP.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Conference of the Parties (COP) is the supreme decision-making body of the United
Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC).
• All States that are Parties to the Convention are represented at the COP, at which they
review the implementation of the Convention.

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding In-situ Bioremediation Techniques:
1. Bioventing is the supply of air and nutrients through wells to contaminated soil to stimulate the growth of indigenous bacteria.
2. In Biosparging, microorganisms are imported to a contaminated site to enhance degradation process.
3. Bioaugmentation is the injection of air under pressure below the water table to increase groundwater oxygen concentrations.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Biosparging is an in-situ remediation technique that supplies oxygen and nutrients to
contaminated soils to promote aerobic biodegradation of contaminants.
• Bioaugmentation is defined as the introduction of cultured microorganisms into a contaminated
environment in order to enhance bioremediation of pollutants.

Not attempted

Which of the following is not a Greenhouse gas?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Greenhouse gases trap heat in the atmosphere, contributing to global warming. Sulfur dioxide
(SO2) is not considered a greenhouse gas because it does not effectively absorb and radiate infrared radiation.

3. The transition zone is the outermost part of a Biosphere reserve.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Human activities, even those intended to have no adverse effects, are strictly prohibited in the
core zone of a biosphere reserve.

Correct answer: D
Solution

pre-formed characters on its surface. As the drum spins, a hammer strikes an ink ribbon against
the paper, transferring the characters.

Q25 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched?
1. VoIP: Use of internet to make phone calls
2. URL: Text used by web browsers to locate a web address on the Internet
3. Unshielded Twisted Pair (UTP) cable: Used to connect computers in a small building
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP), is a technology that allows to make voice calls using a
Internet connection instead of a regular (or analog) phone line.
• A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is a unique identifier used to locate a resource on the
Internet. It is also referred to as a web address.
• UTP cables are used as ethernet cables and telephone wires for short to medium distances to
transfer data and audio signals.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) Technology:
1. Passive tags comprise an internal circuit with a power source, but rely on the radio waves received from the reader to transmit the response.
2. In Semi-passive tags there is no power supply for passive tags.
3. Batteries are being used to power active RFID tags.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Passive RFID tags have no integrated battery or power source. Instead, they draw power
from an RFID reader that sends out electromagnetic waves.
• Semi-passive RFID tags have an internal battery that powers the microchip but still require
an RFID reader’s signal to transmit data..
• Active RFID tags come with an internal battery, allowing them to continuously transmit data
without needing an RFID reader’s signal. This makes them ideal for tracking system
applications where real-time monitoring is required, such as cargo tracking applications and livestock tracking.
Hence, only statement 3 is correct.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about eSign:
1. eSign is an online service for electronic signature without using physical cryptographic token.
2. In eSign, only the thumbprint of the document is submitted for signature function instead of the whole document.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
eSign is an online electronic signature service which can be integrated with service delivery
applications via an open API to facilitate an Aadhaar holder to digitally sign a document, thereby
avoiding physical cryptographic token. It ensures the privacy by requiring that only the thumbprint
(hash) of the document be submitted for signature function instead of the whole document.

Q60 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding semiconductor materials:
1. Mobility of electrons is equal to the mobility of holes.
2. Concentration of electrons or holes in semiconductor material is dependent on doping materials and doping level.
3. In a pure semiconductor material valence band and conduction band are responsible for conduction current.
Which of the above given statements are not correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Since effective mass of holes are greater than that of electrons, thus mobility of electrons is
greater than that of holes.
• The concentration of electrons or holes in semiconductor material is dependent on the type
of dopant (n-type or p-type) and the doping concentration.
• Conduction in pure semiconductor material is dependent on electrons in conduction band
and holes in the valence band.
Thus, statements 1 and 3 are not correct.

Q61 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Ferrites:
1. Resistivity of ferrites is very much lower than that of ferromagnetic materials.
2. Ferrites cored coils are used in high frequency tuned circuits.
3. Ferrites provide high flux density with minimum eddy current loss and very low dielectric loss.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Resistivity of ferrites is higher than that of ferromagnetic material. The higher resistivity of
ferrites minimizes eddy current losses, making them ideal for high-frequency applications like
transformers and inductors. Also, ferrites have a high relative permeability, which allows for
high flux density.

Not attempted

The resistance temperature coefficient of doped semiconductor is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

in decreased resistance. Thus, the resistance temperature coefficient of doped semiconductor is
negative.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Soft magnetic materials have which of the following properties?

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Soft magnetic materials can be easily magnetized and demagnetized. Thus, they have high
permeability (can be easily magnetized), low retentivity i.e. it retains very little magnetism when
the magnetizing field H is removed and low coercivity i.e. low magnetic field required to reduce
the residual magnetization to zero. Since they have a narrow hysteresis loop, the hysteresis
lossses are low.

Q66 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which type of defect has the presence of cations in the interstitial sites?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
In Frenkel defect, small ion displaces from its actual site into the interstitial sites.

Q67 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

What is the number of axis in a hexagonal crystal?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In a hexagonal crystal there are four axis (a1, a2, a3, z). The three a-axes, a1, a2 and a3 , lie in the basal (horizontal) plane, intersect at 120° angles, and are of equal length whereas axis 'c' is perpendicular to the basal plane and has a length that is generally different from the a-axes.

Q68 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is not a necessary condition for fatigue failure ?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Three basic requisites for occurrence of fatigue fracture are: (a) a maximum tensile stress of
sufficiently high value (b) a large enough variation or fluctuation in the applied stress and (c) a
sufficiently large number of cycles of applied stress. Fatigue failure does not have any relation
with recrystallization temperature.

Q69 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Advantage of adding Vanadium in high speed steels (HSS) results in

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The addition of Vanadium in high speed steels (HSS) results in formation of very hard carbides
and thus increases wear resistance.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Addition of silicon to aluminium results in

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Silicon helps in improvement of casting characteristics. Silicon significantly lowers the melting
temperature and increases the fluidity of the molten aluminium.

Q71 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The recrystallization behaviour of a particular metal alloy is specified in terms of recrystallization temperature, which is typically 1/3rd of the absolute melting temperature of a metal or an alloy and depends on several factors including the amount of:
1. Cold working and purity of the metal and alloy.
2. Hot working and purity of the metal and alloy.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Recrystallization temperature of a metal depends on
• Degree of cold working: Higher the degree of cold working, lower is the recrystallization temperature.
• Annealing temperature .
• Impurities in metals: Higher the impurities, difficult is the process of recrystallization.
• High temperature results in faster recrystallization.

In which bank was the disbursement of loans more than 25 percent of all banks in 1986?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Total loan disbursement for 1986 = 203 crores
25% of 203 = 50.75 crores
Bank (I) disbursement is higher than 50.75 crores.

Q90 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

‘A + B’ means ‘A’ is the son of ‘B’; ‘A – B’ means ‘A is the wife of B’; ‘A × B’ means ‘A is the brother of B’; ‘A ÷ B’ means ‘A is the mother of B’ and ‘A = B’ means ‘A is the sister of B’. What does P + R – Q mean?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
P + R – Q means P is the son of R who is the wife of Q i.e. Q is the father of P. Hence, the answer is (a).

Q93 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): WiFi is used for Wireless Wide Area Network (WWAN) only.
Statement (II): WiFi can use 2.4 GHz and 5.0 GHz frequency band.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• WiFi is the technology used primarily for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLANs), which
cover a limited area such as a home, office, or campus.
• WiFi operates in two frequency bands: 2.4 GHz and 5.0 GHz. The 2.4 GHz band offers wider
range but is more susceptible to interference from other devices like cordless phones and
microwave ovens. The 5.0 GHz band offers faster speeds but has shorter range.

Statement (II): EIA is mandatory for all projects in India.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
An Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is not mandatory for all projects in India. It is
mandatory for projects deemed to have a potential for significant environmental impact.

Statement (II): Introduction of sea grasses can reduce the impact of acidification.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Ocean acidification refers to a reduction in the pH of the ocean over an extended period of time,
caused primarily by uptake of carbon dioxide (CO2) from the atmosphere. Nitrogenous compounds
contribute fraction to ocean acidification

Q100 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): MERITE Scheme is designed to implement reforms aligned with the National Education Policy 2020.
Statement (II): It is exclusively for private schools and engineering colleges.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Recently, the Union Cabinet has approved the Multidisciplinary Education and Research Improvement in Technical Education (MERITE) Scheme. It aims to elevate the quality, equity, and governance of technical institutions across the country.
• MERITE Scheme is a Central Sector Scheme designed to implement reforms aligned with the
National Education Policy 2020 (NEP-2020).
• MERITE Scheme aimed to be rolled out in 275 government and government-aided technical
institutions, including 175 engineering colleges; and 100 polytechnic institutes etc.
